title: anthurium scandens subsp. scandens
intro: Anthurium scandens subsp. scandens, also known as the climbing anthurium, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Arum family and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Anthurium scandens subsp. scandens include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve its leaves, which can be made into a tea, or its roots, which can be used to create a tincture or decoction for medicinal purposes.
